title: Fabrication of tri-layered electrospun polycaprolactone mats with improved sustained drug release profile.
notes: The curve represents cumulative release of ibuprofen over time, suitable for digitization.

title: Hard polymeric porous microneedles on stretchable substrate for transdermal drug delivery.
notes: The subplot focuses on UV-Vis spectroscopy data for ibuprofen, clearly depicting the absorption spectra at varying concentrations. Although multiple curves are present, subplot A is the most relevant for digitization of endpoint curves.

title: Ibuprofen inhibits cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator-mediated Cl- secretion.
notes: Panel B shows a clear concentration-response curve for ibuprofen inhibition on I_sc, making it the best choice for triage.

title: Synthesis and application of magnetic@layered double hydroxide as an anti-inflammatory drugs nanocarrier.
notes: This page contains relevant data on ibuprofen release at various time points. It appears to be the most complete panel describing the cumulative release kinetics.

title: Effects of Temperature and Humidity on the Skin Permeation of Hydrophilic and Hydrophobic Drugs.
notes: Subplot A represents the cumulative amounts (Q) of IP (IP: ideal penetration behavior) that permeated through excised porcine skin under different environmental conditions. Other plots depict similar information for ANP which may lead to ambiguity in the context of overall assessment.

title: Transcutol® P/Cremophor® EL/Ethyl Oleate-Formulated Microemulsion Loaded into Hyaluronic Acid-Based Hydrogel for Improved Transdermal Delivery and Biosafety of Ibuprofen.

notes: Focus on graph (A) showing cumulative amount of Lidocaine and Ibuprofen; it clearly presents relevant endpoint curves.

title: Enhanced neurite outgrowth of human model (NT2) neurons by small-molecule inhibitors of Rho/ROCK signaling.
notes: Panel A demonstrates a clear dose-response relationship for neurite length with various concentrations of Y-27632, making it the best target for analysis. Panel B, although relevant, follows a different treatment (Fasudil) and distracts from the primary focus.

title: Ibuprofen inhibits colitis-induced overexpression of tumor-related Rac1b.
notes: Subplot A presents a clear endpoint curve for ibuprofen and its effects on Rac1b mRNA levels, making it ideal for digitization.

title: A new vesicle-loaded hydrogel system suitable for topical applications: preparation and characterization.
notes: Figure 2 contains two subplots, but subplot A presents relevant ibuprofen release data in a clear cumulative format. Subplot B focuses on a different aspect of released data and is therefore less relevant.

title: Compatibilizing of cotton fabric with hydrophobic drug cover layer for anti-inflammatory performance with the implementation of ibuprofen.
notes: Figure 3 is the only one presenting an endpoint curve related to ibuprofen release.

title: Green Fabrication and Release Mechanisms of pH-Sensitive Chitosan-Ibuprofen Aerogels for Controlled Transdermal Delivery of Ibuprofen.
notes: The subplot shows the FTIR spectrum of ibuprofen, which may contain relevant endpoint information. However, it is more focused on spectral analysis rather than a release curve.

title: Hyaluronic acid-ibuprofen conjugation: a novel ototherapeutic approach protecting inner ear cells from inflammation-mediated damage.
Inflammatory Role of ROS-Sensitive AMP-Activated Protein Kinase in the Hypersensitivity of Lung Vagal C Fibers Induced by Intermittent Hypoxia in Rats.
notes: Subplot A clearly reflects the effects of capsaicin in both RA and IH groups, making it the best choice. Other panels also show important data, but A stands out for its clarity in demonstrating endpoint curves.

title: In Situ Generation of Fouling Resistant Ag/Pd Modified PES Membranes for Treatment of Pharmaceutical Wastewater.
